descriptions: Morello family member Eugene Ubriaco and Nicholas Morello met with the Navy Street gang and were killed.
edges.evidence_quote: On September 7, 1916, Ubriaco along with Nicholas Morello meet with the Navy Street gang in Brooklyn and they both were shot to death on Johnson Street in Brooklyn.

descriptions: The two Camorra gangs cooperated against the Morello family.
edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets

descriptions: The Navy Street gang and Coney Island gang were in conflict with the Morello family.
edges.evidence_quote: Both gangs initially worked together against the Morello family from Italian Harlem for control of the lucrative New York rackets

ETA.description: ETA was an armed Basque nationalist and separatist organization active in Spain and France between 1959 and 2018. Founded in 1959 during Francoist Spain by Basque nationalist students, it evolved into a clandestine paramilitary group conducting assassinations, bombings, and kidnappings primarily in Spain. ETA killed over 800 people and was classified as a terrorist group by multiple countries. It announced a definitive end to armed activity in 2011 and formally dissolved in 2018.

descriptions: ETA trafficked cocaine from FARC and traded it with the Mafia for guns.
edges.evidence_quote: Italian author and mafia specialist Roberto Saviano pointed to a relationship of the group with the Mafia. According to this view, ETA trafficked cocaine which it got via its FARC contacts, then traded it with the Mafia for guns.

descriptions: ATE was an anti-ETA group.
edges.evidence_quote: Anti-ETA groups: ATE (Anti-Terrorismo ETA)

descriptions: Batallón Vasco Español conducted similar dirty war actions against ETA before GAL.
edges.evidence_quote: GAL activities were a follow-up of similar dirty war actions by death squads, actively supported by members of Spanish security forces and secret services, using names such as Batallón Vasco Español active from 1975 to 1981.

descriptions: Batallón Vasco Español was an anti-ETA group.
edges.evidence_quote: Anti-ETA groups: Batallón Vasco Español

descriptions: ETA shared intelligence and provided training to Breton Liberation Front.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA had extensive ties with the Breton Liberation Front – both organizations shared intelligence, and Breton revolutionaries received training from ETA in the Basque movement's clandestine camps in the Pyrenees.

descriptions: ETA and Breton Revolutionary Army teamed up to steal explosives.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1999 ETA militants teamed up with the (now self-dissolved) Breton Revolutionary Army to steal explosives from magazines in Brittany.

descriptions: ETA originated from the student group Ekin.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA grew out of a student group called Ekin, founded in the early 1950s

descriptions: Ekin was accused of promoting civil disobedience in connection with ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: A trial against the political organization Ekin (Action), accused of promoting civil disobedience.

descriptions: FLB-ARB is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure

descriptions: ETA exchanged weapons and provided logistical support to FP-25.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA exchanged weapons, explosives and provided logistical support to the Forças Populares 25 de Abril (FP-25), a Portuguese far-left terrorist group.

descriptions: GAL conducted a dirty war against ETA, killing ETA members and exiles.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1980s, a 'dirty war' ensued using the Grupos Antiterroristas de Liberación (GAL, 'Antiterrorist Liberation Groups'), a paramilitary group which billed themselves as counter-terrorist, active between 1983 and 1987. The GAL's stated mission was to avenge every ETA killing with another killing of ETA exiles

descriptions: ETA and GAL were adversaries in the Basque conflict, with GAL targeting ETA members and ETA attacking GAL-associated targets.
edges.evidence_quote: These were the first systematic demonstrations in the Basque Country against political violence. Also in 1986, in Ordizia, ETA gunned down María Dolores Katarain...

descriptions: GAL was an anti-ETA group.
edges.evidence_quote: Anti-ETA groups: Grupos Antiterroristas de Liberación (GAL)

descriptions: Guerrilleros de Cristo Rey was an anti-ETA group.
edges.evidence_quote: Anti-ETA groups: Guerrilleros de Cristo Rey

descriptions: KAS was an ETA-affiliated party that listed conditions for ETA to stop armed struggle.
edges.evidence_quote: One of the parties within Herri Batasuna, ETA-affiliated KAS, listed five conditions from ETA

edges.target: Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária
descriptions: LUAR brokered weapons purchases for ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1960s, the Portuguese group Liga de Unidade e Ação Revolucionária (LUAR), which was fighting the dictatorship, brokered the contacts that allowed ETA to purchase weapons in the then Czechoslovak Republic.

descriptions: Provisional IRA is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure

descriptions: ETA and IRA had fraternal contacts and parallel struggles.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA was known to have had 'fraternal' contacts with the Provisional Irish Republican Army; the two groups have both, at times, characterized their struggles as parallel.

descriptions: FARC is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure

descriptions: ETA had contacts with FARC; ETA members received training from FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: The Colombian government stated that there are contacts between ETA and the Colombian guerrillas FARC. The recent capture of FARC's leaders' computers, and leaked email exchanges between both groups, show that ETA members received training from FARC.

descriptions: Terra Lliure is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure

descriptions: ETA cooperated with the Catalan nationalist movement, which included Terra Lliure.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA cooperated with the Catalan nationalist movement, which had its own far-left separatist organization that based itself off ETA – Terra Lliure

descriptions: Terra Lliure was an ally of ETA; they coordinated actions and supported each other.
edges.evidence_quote: A Catalan revolutionary organization that based itself off ETA, Terra Lliure, was an ally of ETA, and both organizations coordinated their actions and supported each other.

descriptions: Xaki was described as ETA's international network.
edges.evidence_quote: A trial against a little-known organization called Xaki, acquitted in 2001 as the 'international network' of ETA.

descriptions: ETA is listed as an ally of the Provisional IRA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NORAID (Irish Americans) , Libya , PLO , ETA , FARC

descriptions: The IRA and ETA exchanged weapons and training.
edges.evidence_quote: The IRA had links with the Basque separatist group ETA. Maria McGuire states the IRA received fifty revolvers from ETA in exchange for explosives training.

descriptions: FARC is allied with ETA.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Euskadi Ta Askatasuna

descriptions: North Side Gang originated from the Market Street Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: the North Side Gang originated from the Market Street Gang

descriptions: Boundary is in alliance with or subsumed into Mali Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Many smaller local gangs are in alliance or have been subsumed into the Mali Boys, such as Drive & Marlowe (DM), Boundary, Coppermill, and Stoneydown.

descriptions: The Trovato have an alliance with the Crisafulli clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleanza con il clan Crisafulli di Quarto Oggiaro

descriptions: Indian police claimed PAFF is an offshoot of Jaish-e-Muhammad.
edges.evidence_quote: The Indian police claimed it is an offshoot of Jaish-e-Muhammad.

descriptions: People's Anti-Fascist Front is listed as a non-state ally of Lashkar-e-Taiba.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Jaish-e-Mohammed, Pakistani Taliban, Hizbul Mujahideen, Harkat-ul-Jihad al-Islami, Harkat-ul-Mujahideen, Al-Badr, Al Rashid Trust, Indian Mujahideen, People’s Anti-Fascist Front, Muslim United Liberation Tigers of Assam, Al-Qaeda AQIS AGH

descriptions: PAFF was originally thought to be a faction of Lashkar-e-Taiba.
edges.evidence_quote: The PAFF was originally thought to be a faction of Lashkar-e-Taiba according to Indian officials.

descriptions: Clan Carminati had its roots in Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 dicembre 2014 l'ex NAR Massimo Carminati, vicino alla Banda già dalla fine degli anni ‘70, viene arrestato assieme ad alcuni complici nell'ambito dell'operazione Mafia Capitale ; chiamata come Cupola Romana o ancora Clan Carminati , questa organizzazione operava a Roma già a partire dal 2000 circa, affondando le sue radici nelle rapine dei NAR degli anni ottanta e successivamente nella stessa banda della Magliana.

descriptions: The Bath Avenue Crew is a farm team for the Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: DeFrancisci is a founding member of the Bath Avenue Crew, the infamous Bonanno farm team.

descriptions: Bath Avenue crew is a subgroup of the Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: former associate with the Bath Avenue crew, a follower and subgroup of the Bonanno crime family.

descriptions: The Bath Avenue Crew is a crew operating under the Bonanno family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bath Avenue Crew operated in the Bensonhurst section of Brooklyn, New York City

Fullerton Boys.description: A Korean-American street gang based in Fullerton, California, founded in 1996. Members were originally from other gangs such as LVM, SZA, and Wah Ching. The gang emerged as a collective identity after other gangs disbanded in Fullerton.

descriptions: Fullerton Boys members were originally from LVM.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of its members belonged to the LVM gang, SZA gang (Sarzana) gang and some from the Wah Ching gang.

descriptions: Fullerton Boys members were originally from SZA.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of its members belonged to the LVM gang, SZA gang (Sarzana) gang and some from the Wah Ching gang.

'Ndrina Sia.description: The 'Ndrina Sia is a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Soverato, Calabria, Italy, with influence in neighboring towns. It is allied with several other 'ndrine and has been involved in a violent feud from 2008 to 2010 that resulted in about twenty deaths. The organization is involved in drug trafficking and has connections in Rome, Milan, and Turin. Recent operations have targeted its leadership and assets.

descriptions: The Sia are listed as allies of the Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Sia

descriptions: The Sia 'ndrina is allied with the Costa 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei ... i Costa di Siderno

descriptions: A feud erupted between the Sia-Procopio-Novella-Vallelunga alliance and the Gallace-Ruga-Metastasio alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il 2008 e il 2010 è scoppiata una faida tra i Sia-Procopio- Novella - Vallelunga e i Gallace - Ruga-Metastasio

descriptions: The Sia 'ndrina is allied with the Gallace 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei ... i Gallace di Guardavalle

descriptions: The Sia 'ndrina is allied with the Procopio-Lentini 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei ... i Procopio-Lentini di Satriano e Davoli

descriptions: The Sia 'ndrina is allied with the Vallelunga 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei Vallelunga di Serra San Bruno

descriptions: The Sia alliance included Novella in the feud against Gallace.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il 2008 e il 2010 è scoppiata una faida tra i Sia-Procopio- Novella - Vallelunga e i Gallace - Ruga-Metastasio

descriptions: The Sia alliance feuded with the Ruga-Metastasio alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il 2008 e il 2010 è scoppiata una faida tra i Sia-Procopio- Novella - Vallelunga e i Gallace - Ruga-Metastasio

descriptions: Vallelunga and Sia were allies against Gallace-Ruga-Metastasio in the second boschi feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Seconda faida dei boschi : scoppiata nel 2008 e conclusa nel 2013 ... tra i Vallelunga , Novella , Sia , Tripodi, Procopio e Lentini contro i Gallace , Ruga e Metastasio.

descriptions: The Sia were part of the Vallelunga locale before the 2009 faida.
edges.evidence_quote: il locale guidato da Damiano Vallelunga era formato da sette 'ndrine operanti in sette paesi diversi: ... i Sia a Soverato

descriptions: Grande Aracri helped the Valerio family in a shooting attack.
edges.evidence_quote: sparatoria a colpi di armi da guerra ... fatta partire dalla 'Ndrina della famiglia "Valerio" ... aiutati dai loro alleati (Grande Aracri)

descriptions: Famiglia Valerio is listed as an allied 'ndrina in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Famiglia Valerio (Cutro)

Kings Crew Motorcycle Club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Western Canada, founded in Calgary, Alberta in 1977. It became one of the province's most dominant clubs, participating in the Alberta Biker Conflict. The club eventually joined the Hells Angels in the late 1990s through a patch-over ceremony.

descriptions: Kings Crew and Grim Reapers were rivals, engaged in violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Grim Reapers

descriptions: Kings Crew merged into Hells Angels via patch-over.
edges.evidence_quote: until eventually joining the Hell's Angels in the late 1990s.

descriptions: Lobos listed as ally of Kings Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Lobos

descriptions: Kings Crew and Rebels were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rebels Motorcycle Club

descriptions: Warlocks listed as rival of Kings Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Warlocks Motorcycle Club (Florida)

descriptions: Kings Crew listed as an ally of Rebels in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Satan's Choice , Kings Crew Motorcycle Club , Lobos and, later, the Hells Angels

descriptions: The Qarajah family had a blood feud with the Jarushi family, resulting in deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: The Qarajah crime family, also originating from Ramla, used to have a long time blood feud with the Jarushis, resulting in the death of 24 people in the 1990.

Universal Aryan Brotherhood.description: Active neo-Nazi white supremacist prison gang in the United States, primarily based in Oklahoma. Founded in 1993 by Wilhelm Mast within the Oklahoma Department of Corrections. The gang operates both inside and outside prison walls, engaging in racketeering, kidnapping, murder, drug trafficking, and other crimes. It has no connection to the original Aryan Brotherhood.

descriptions: UAB is allied with Aryan Brotherhood of Mississippi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Brotherhood of Mississippi

descriptions: UAB has Aryan Circle as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Circle, Oklahoma Irish Mob Gang

descriptions: UAB has Oklahoma Irish Mob Gang as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Circle, Oklahoma Irish Mob Gang

descriptions: Two Greco factions fought each other in the 1940s.
edges.evidence_quote: Michele Greco, leader of a different faction of the Greco clan hailing from Croceverde Giardini, which had fought its own war with the Ciaculli Grecos in the 1940s.

descriptions: The two Greco clans were historically at odds, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Historically, the Greco clan from Croceverde Giardini had been at odds with the Greco clan of Ciaculli led by Salvatore 'Ciaschiteddu' Greco, of whom Bontade was an ally.

descriptions: The text lists Outcast Motorcycle Club as an enemy of Wheels of Soul.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemies of the Wheels of Soul include the Outcast Motorcycle Club, Sin City Titans, Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club and the Street Soldiers.

Maceo Organization.description: The Maceo Organization was a criminal syndicate that controlled Galveston, Texas, from the 1920s to the 1980s. Founded by brothers Sam and Rosario Maceo, it engaged in illegal gambling, prostitution, bootlegging, and racketeering. The organization wielded significant political influence, with ties to Texas Governor James V. Allred and other officials. It was eventually succeeded by the Fertitta Organization.

descriptions: Quinn's Beach Gang mentored and partnered with the Maceos, who later succeeded Quinn's organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Quinn became a mentor, and then a partner, to Sam and Rosario Maceo , who later took over the Galveston underworld.

descriptions: The Maceo Syndicate, successors to the Beach Gang, ordered the assassination of Downtown Gang member George Musey.
edges.evidence_quote: George Musey was assassinated through orders by the Beach Gang's successors, the Maceo Syndicate

descriptions: The Maceo Organization evolved from the Beach Gang, with Quinn and Voight becoming partners.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Beach Gang

descriptions: The Maceo Organization was a rival of the Downtown Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Downtown Gang

descriptions: The Fertitta Group succeeded the Maceo Organization after the Maceo brothers' deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: After Sam and Rose's deaths in the early 1950s, the brothers were succeeded by the Fertitta Group

descriptions: Gente Nueva Salazar is a faction supported by the original Gente Nueva.
edges.evidence_quote: la Gente Nueva original se mantuvo liderando a La Gente Nueva en Agua Prieta apoyando a otras facciones como Gente Nueva Salazar, Gente Nueva del Tigre, y Gente Nueva del Jaguar Arvizu, contra La Línea y contra la Gente Nueva de Los Salgueiro

descriptions: Gente Nueva Salazar fights against La Línea.
edges.evidence_quote: la Gente Nueva original se mantuvo liderando a La Gente Nueva en Agua Prieta apoyando a otras facciones como Gente Nueva Salazar, Gente Nueva del Tigre, y Gente Nueva del Jaguar Arvizu, contra La Línea

Genovese crime family New Jersey faction.description: The New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters controlling organized crime in New Jersey. It is divided into multiple crews involved in labor racketeering, illegal gambling, loansharking, and extortion. The faction has maintained a strong presence in Northern Jersey since the early Prohibition era. Notable members include Guarino 'Willie' Moretti, Gerardo 'Jerry' Catena, and Louis 'Bobby' Manna.

edges.target: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction is a faction of the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction operates throughout New Jersey.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: Genovese New Jersey faction and Gambino family had a conflict over territory and leadership, with Manna plotting to kill Gotti.
edges.evidence_quote: Manna was displeased over John Gotti's unsanctioned hit of Castellano and his accession as new boss, but he continued discussing the takeover of Bruno's operations. By 1987, the relationship between the two families had deteriorated.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: A Genovese faction soldier was involved in a bribery case with a Gambino soldier.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1975, Doto Jr. was charged with bribery after Gambino family soldier Anthony Carminati was arrested.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction is a subgroup of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters who control organized crime activities within the state of New Jersey.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction is a part of the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters who control organized crime activities within the state of New Jersey.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction cooperated with the Lucchese family through a working relationship between Fiumara and Accetturo.
edges.evidence_quote: He also maintained a working relationship with Lucchese Jersey capo Anthony 'Tumac' Accetturo.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction was originally controlled by the Masseria family.
edges.evidence_quote: Early members of the New Jersey faction were under the control of New York City's Masseria family boss Joe Masseria.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: Genovese New Jersey faction planned to take over Philadelphia family's operations after Bruno's murder.
edges.evidence_quote: The killing prompted discussions and plans by the Genovese and Gambino families to take over Bruno's criminal operations.

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.target: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The infobox lists Genovese (New Jersey faction) as an ally of the New Jersey faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Gambino, Genovese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia

Al Salam 313.description: Political organization and criminal gang of conservative Shia Iraqis in Western Europe, founded in Germany by Mohammed Bunia. Modeled after a motorcycle club, it threatens secular Iraqis in Europe and is ideologically linked to the Iraqi Shia militia Sarāyā al-Salām. Active in Germany, Sweden, Denmark, and the Netherlands.

descriptions: Al Salam 313 is ideologically based on Sarāyā al-Salām.
edges.evidence_quote: The Salam 313 has members in Central and North Europe and is based on the ideology of the Iraqi Shia militia Sarāyā al-Salām (former Mahdi Army).

descriptions: Nuovo Ordine Sociale is the Italian chapter of Atomwaffen Division.
edges.evidence_quote: Atomwaffen had a full-fledged chapter in Italy with 38 members, formed in Savona. The group was called Nuovo Ordine Sociale

descriptions: Nuovo Ordine Sociale-Sole Nero is a splinter group of Nuovo Ordine Sociale.
edges.evidence_quote: a splinter group of the Italian Atomwaffen "Nuovo Ordine Sociale-Sole Nero"

Pittsburgh crime family.description: The Pittsburgh crime family, also known as the LaRocca crime family, was an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. Formed in the late 1880s under Salvatore Catanzaro, it rose to prominence during Prohibition and was led for decades by Sebastian LaRocca. The family engaged in racketeering, labor union infiltration, and drug trafficking, but became defunct after convictions and attrition, with the last known member dying in 2021.

descriptions: Bufalino family listed as ally of Pittsburgh crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Pittsburgh crime family

descriptions: Cleveland family had a profit-sharing arrangement with the Pittsburgh family over Youngstown rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: an arrangement with the Pittsburgh crime family which entitled the Cleveland family to twenty-five percent of the Pittsburgh family's profits from rackets in Youngstown

descriptions: The Pittsburgh crime family is listed as an ally of the Detroit Partnership.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Pittsburgh crime family

descriptions: Genovese family lists Pittsburgh as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family, Bufalino crime family, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Colombo crime family, Dallas crime family, DeCavalcante crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Lucchese crime family, New Orleans crime family, Patriarca crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Trafficante crime family, Hells Angels MC, Latin Kings, Pagan's MC, Purple Gang

descriptions: Pittsburgh family was represented by the Genovese family on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Pittsburgh crime family

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Nations, Bandidos MC, Black Pistons MC, Breed MC, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Detroit Partnership, Grim Reapers MC, Gypsy Joker MC, Hammerskins, New Orleans crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Rock Machine MC

descriptions: The Outlaws have been criminally associated with the Pittsburgh crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The club has also been criminally associated with the Buffalo, Chicago, Detroit, New Orleans and Pittsburgh crime families

descriptions: Youngstown Outlaws chapter cooperated with Pittsburgh crime family in various criminal activities.
edges.evidence_quote: The Youngstown Outlaws chapter has been associated with the Pittsburgh crime family in contract killing, witness intimidation, debt collection, drug dealing, arson and assaults.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Pittsburgh crime family

descriptions: The Pittsburgh family listed the Bufalino family as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bufalino crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Genovese crime family, Kansas City crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Rochester crime family, Trafficante crime family, Outlaws MC, Pagan's MC

descriptions: Pittsburgh family capo Mannarino had ties to Bufalino family.
edges.evidence_quote: Gabriel "Kelly" Mannarino – former capo who controlled the New Kensington rackets. Mannarino had ties to the Bufalino family.

descriptions: The Pittsburgh family listed the Chicago Outfit as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bufalino crime family, Chicago Outfit, Cleveland crime family, Detroit Partnership, Gambino crime family, Genovese crime family, Kansas City crime family, Philadelphia crime family, Rochester crime family, Trafficante crime family, Outlaws MC, Pagan's MC

descriptions: Pittsburgh family fought a mob war with Cleveland family over Youngstown rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: Vincent 'Two-Gun Jimmy' Prato, the local caporegime from the Pittsburgh family, attempted to establish a monopoly over gambling and extortion rackets in Youngstown, a territory which had historically been shared between the two families. This resulted in a mob war

descriptions: The Pittsburgh family was in conflict with the Cleveland family over expansion into Youngstown.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1960s, LaRocca's family engaged in conflict with the Cleveland crime family when they expanded into Youngstown, Ohio.

descriptions: The Pittsburgh family partnered with the Cleveland crime family in rackets in Northeast Ohio.
edges.evidence_quote: Under LaRocca's leadership, the crime family became powerful in Pittsburgh's labor unions and established rackets in Northeast Ohio, in which it partnered with the Cleveland crime family.

descriptions: Pittsburgh family capo Ferruccio paid tribute to Cleveland family.
edges.evidence_quote: Pasquale "Pat" Ferruccio – a former capo who operated in Canton-Stark County, Ohio area and the Akron-Summit County, Ohio area while paying tribute to the Cleveland family.

descriptions: Pittsburgh family shared illegal income with Cleveland family.
edges.evidence_quote: He established rackets in Ohio, while sharing some of the illegal income with the Cleveland crime family.

descriptions: The Pittsburgh family's Youngstown faction shared gambling profits with the Cleveland crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: During the early 1970s the faction gained control of the gambling rackets in Youngstown and shared some of the profits with the Cleveland crime family.

descriptions: Pittsburgh family formed agreement with Tampa family to operate casinos in Havana.
edges.evidence_quote: LaRocca also formed an agreement with Tampa crime family boss Santo Trafficante operating casinos in Havana, Cuba.

descriptions: Angelo Marino's father was a member of the Pittsburgh family, indicating a familial link between the families.
edges.evidence_quote: His father, Salvatore, a longtime member of the Pittsburgh crime family, handed the company to Marino.

descriptions: Trafficante and Pittsburgh family jointly controlled a casino in Havana.
edges.evidence_quote: Trafficante also entered into an agreement with Pittsburgh crime family boss John LaRocca to jointly control the Sans Souci casino in Havana.

descriptions: Listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Celtic Club

descriptions: Bakassi Freedom Fighters is the radical armed faction of BAMOSD.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha participado en acciones de secuestro y terrorismo sobre todo por el Bakassi Freedom Fighters , facción armada rádical del grupo

descriptions: BAMOSD receives support from NDDSC.
edges.evidence_quote: recibe apoyo de la SCAPO y del NDDSC

descriptions: BAMOSD receives support from SCAPO.
edges.evidence_quote: recibe apoyo de la SCAPO y del NDDSC

descriptions: D'Agostino acted as a link between Serraino-Condello-Imerti and Commisso-Cordì-Aquino.
edges.evidence_quote: l'anello di congiunzione tra le cosche reggine facenti capo ai Serraino-Condello-Imerti e quelle della Locride (Commisso-Cordì-Aquino)

descriptions: Peaky Blinders fought the Sloggers for territory in Birmingham.
edges.evidence_quote: The Blinders' dominance came about from beating rivals, including the "Sloggers", "a pugilistic term for someone who could strike a heavy blow in the ring", whom they fought for territory in Birmingham and its surrounding districts.

descriptions: Black Jackets and Dogpack 416 are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rock Machine , Veles gang , Black Dogs , Bandogs , 210 International , Dogpack 416 , Black Army , Holmblaaa , Kommando 210

descriptions: The Casamonica and Morelli families clashed violently in 1956.
edges.evidence_quote: Da una parte la famiglia Casamonica contrapposta alla famiglia Morelli, che si scontrano con coltelli, roncole, bastoni e fruste.

descriptions: The Morelli and Spada families fought against the Sarachella family in 1954.
edges.evidence_quote: un gruppo composto dalle famiglie Morelli e Spada si scontra con un gruppo di un'altra famiglia nomade del posto, i Sarachella.

descriptions: Wheels of Soul engaged in a shootout with Ghetto Riders in 1979.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 22, 1979, Wheels of Soul was involved in a shootout at Woodrow Wilson High School in Camden, New Jersey against rival gang Ghetto Riders.

'Ndrina Paparo.description: The 'Ndrina Paparo is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Calabrian mafia organization, based in Isola Capo Rizzuto, Italy. It is allied with the Nicoscia, Arena, and Barbaro clans and has a branch in the Milan area. The clan has been involved in mafia association, extortion, arms trafficking, and public works corruption, including the high-speed railway line between Milan and Venice. In 2009, a major operation led to multiple arrests and seizures of assets worth €10 million.

descriptions: Paparo are allies of Nicoscia and Arena.
edges.evidence_quote: tra questi i Paparo, alleati dei Nicoscia e degli Arena

descriptions: The Barbaro are linked to the Paparo 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono legati alla cosca dei Papalia e dei Paparo.

descriptions: Bubbo sold weapons to Paparo.
edges.evidence_quote: Avrebbero venduto delle armi ai Paparo

descriptions: The Paparo are allies of the Nicoscia.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 16 marzo 2009, i carabinieri di Sesto San Giovanni ( MI ), nell'operazione Isola , iniziata nel 2004, hanno ordinato l'arresto, per vari reati, di più di 20 persone a Milano , Crotone , Catanzaro e Taranto tra cui associazione a delinquere di stampo mafioso, detenzione e porto illegale di armi, estorsione e tentato omicidio, tra questi i Paparo , alleati dei Nicoscia e degli Arena, accusati di sfruttamento dell'immigrazione, riciclaggio, violenza privata, favoreggiamento di latitanti e atti d'intimidazione nella costruzione delle grandi opere pubbliche della Lombardia , come alcune tratte dell'alta velocità ferroviaria Milano - Venezia .

descriptions: Paparo is a sub-unit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Paparo sarebbe una 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: Paparo is allied with Arena.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata ... degli Arena

descriptions: Paparo is allied with Barbaro.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata ... dei Barbaro

descriptions: Paparo is allied with Nicoscia.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata dei Nicoscia

descriptions: Redline Crew is a Hells Angels puppet club.
edges.evidence_quote: a number of puppet clubs were formed such as ... the Redline Crew in Toronto

descriptions: Necurs is listed as an author of Dridex, which is an alias of Evil Corp.
edges.evidence_quote: Authors: Necurs , Maksim Yakubets

descriptions: Rusich founders graduated from a paramilitary training program run by the Russian Imperial Legion.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was founded by Alexey Milchakov and Yan Petrovsky, both of them neo-Nazis, in the summer of 2014, after graduating from a paramilitary training program run by the Russian Imperial Legion

descriptions: The Parlor Mob was an ally of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club , Parlor Mob , Gorillas , Rhodes Gang

descriptions: PYG and Lettsom Boys engaged in a shootout.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2014, a man was sentenced to a minimum of 35 years in prison for the shooting of Rio MacFarlane in Peckham. The victim was an innocent bystander in a shootout between the PYG and Lettsom Boys.
